title: Brassica mesopotamica
taxonomy: Brassica mesopotamica is classified within the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. This classification is based on the plant's botanical characteristics and its genetic relationships with other species in the same family. The genus Brassica includes a diverse range of species, many of which are cultivated for their edible leaves, flowers, or seeds. Brassica mesopotamica is a wild species, and its classification is subject to ongoing research and revision in the field of botany. Further study is needed to fully understand the taxonomic relationships and classification of this plant within the Brassicaceae family.
